child_geo_events.address: 1350 S Sepulveda Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90025, USA
child_geo_events.name: Evacuation Shelter - Westwood Recreation Center

data.information:
An Evacuation Shelter is located at Westwood Recreation Center, per LAFD. Small animals are accepted. Residents may remain overnight.

LADWP will be distributing bottled water to affected customers at the Westwood Recreation Center. The distribution is open 24 hours in coordination with American Red Cross.

child_geo_events.address: 300 E Green St, Pasadena, CA 91101, USA
child_geo_events.name: Evacuation Shelter - Pasadena Convention Center

data.information:
An evacuation shelter is available at the Pasadena Convention Center Exhibition Hall, 300 East Green Street, Pasadena, CA 91101 per the LA County website.

Parking is available at the parking structure across the street from the Convention Center, located at 155 E. Green St. Please enter from Marengo Ave.

Parking is also available at the Paseo parking structure, located at 360 E. Colorado Blvd. You are able to enter the structure from Colorado Blvd. or Green St.

The Humane Society is on-site to accept pets for boarding. Service animals will be allowed inside the Convention Center.

Residents are able to call 211 for information regarding shelters, and social and health services.

child_geo_events.name: WCK Meal Distribution - Fraternal Order of Eagles

data.information:
World Central Kitchen (WCK) is serving free meals to those affected by the Eaton Fire and Palisades fires, including first responders, at the following location 455 E Woodbury Rd, Altadena, CA 91001, USA

Time of service is from 11am to 3pm today, February 2nd.

child_geo_events.address: 15045 Sunset Blvd, Pacific Palisades, CA 90272, USA
child_geo_events.name: WCK Meal Distribution - Palisades Fire Station 69

data.information:
World Central Kitchen (WCK) is serving free meals to those affected, including first responders, at Palisades Fire Station 69 located at 15045 Sunset Blvd, Pacific Palisades, CA 90272, USA.

Time of service is from 11am to 8pm today, February 2nd.

child_geo_events.address: 540 W Woodbury Rd, Altadena, CA 91001, USA
child_geo_events.name: Disaster Recovery Center

data.information:
A new Disaster Recovery Center will be opening in Altadena on Monday, Jan. 27, 2025, from 9 AM to 8 PM and will be available to residents seven days a week.
The new center will be located at 540 West Woodbury Road, Altadena, CA 91001 per

LA County Recovers

Eaton Fire Final Update: As a result of all evacuations being lifted and the fire now being 100% contained, the Eaton Fire is being deactivated from Watch Duty. The archived incident can continue to be accessed at https://app.watchduty.org/i/40388, but the icon will no longer be visible on the map.

World Central Kitchen meal distribution locations will continue to be updated on the Watch Duty map. To view the operating hours of each location, click on it to access the “Info” section, where you’ll find the listed opening and closing times. These hours are updated daily around midnight.

For emailed updates on the recovery and rebuilding process, please see the bottom of this page on the LA County Recovers website. These emails include daily updates and information on upcoming community meetings.

A list of additional resources is available on the LA County Recovers website and the 211 LA website. Resources specifically for impacted workers and businesses is available on the LA County Department of Economic Opportunity website.

2 Disaster Recovery Centers will remain open from 9am to 8pm every day. Over 75 agencies are present to connect those affected with resources to help start the recovery process. Those affected are welcome to come over multiple days; there’s no rush to complete everything in one day. The locations are listed below:

  1. UCLA Research Park West - 10850 West Pico Blvd., Los Angeles, CA 90064

  2. Altadena Disaster Recovery Center - 540 W Woodbury Rd. Altadena, Ca 91001

The deadline to participate in the free private property debris cleanup by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers is March 31st, 2025. For more information and to fill out the right of entry form, see https://recovery.lacounty.gov/debris-removal/.

A portion of Angeles National Forest is closed through December 31st, 2025 for public safety and natural recovery of the landscape. Learn more at https://www.fs.usda.gov/alerts/angeles/alerts-notices/?aid=93079.

child_geo_events.address: 695 W Sierra Madre Blvd, Sierra Madre, CA 91024, USA
child_geo_events.name: WCK Meal Distrubtion - Sierra Madre United Methodist

data.information:
World Central Kitchen (WCK) is serving free meals to those affected, including first responders, at the Sierra Madre United Methodist Church located at 695 W Sierra Madre Blvd, Sierra Madre, CA 91024, USA.

Time of service is from 4pm to 8pm today, February 3rd.

Additionally, World Central Kitchen (WCK) is offering free water refills of bottles and other large containers to evacuees and first responders at this location. Free 5 gallon bladders will also be available.
Time of Service: 4:00pm - 8:00pm

child_geo_events.address: 1150 Pacific Coast Hwy, Santa Monica, CA 90403
child_geo_events.name: Resident Access Pass & WCK Meal Distribution - Lot 3 North

data.information:
All zones in the City of Los Angeles are now open for resident-only access, per LAFD.

Residents can check in with law enforcement at Beach Parking Lot 3 North (1150 Pacific Coast Hwy, Santa Monica, CA 90401, USA) between 10:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. for an access pass to all Resident-Only Zones in the City of Los Angeles. Access passes are required; however, escorts are no longer needed. Curfew hours remain in effect.

Contractors will be allowed in the fire area (LA City) to conduct assessment work. Contractors will be required to provide identification and proof that they have been hired by specific residents within the impacted areas. Contractors must report to the check-in area (Lot 3) to be granted access and will be provided a pass to enter.

Additionally, World Central Kitchen (WCK) is serving free meals to those affected, including first responders, at Beach Parking Lot 3 North located at 1150 Pacific Coast Hwy, Santa Monica, CA 90403. Time of service is from 11am to 4pm February 3rd.
